---
title: Additional Material
---

## Purpose

Use this page for optional additions when you have extra time, a more confident cohort, or a strong local example to work with. The best extensions still deepen judgement rather than simply adding more AI content.

## Optional Extensions by Theme

### How AI changes research work

- Ask groups to map where AI reduces effort and where it removes natural pauses for reflection.
- Compare a research task completed manually, with AI support, and with over-reliance on AI.
- Use a short case where speed improves output quantity but weakens checking discipline.

### Documentation and workflows

- Show a lightweight AI use note and ask what is missing.
- Workflow design activity: where should AI help, where should it stop, and where should documentation happen?
- Compare a sensible narrow agent idea with one that automates the wrong kind of judgement.

## Optional Demonstrations

- Demonstrate how a stronger prompt improves usefulness more than a role prompt alone.
- Show how AI output changes when you move from "answer from memory" to "use only this source".
- Walk through a simple policy-checking workflow from source to summary to manual validation.

## Useful Artefacts to Prepare

If you want richer discussion, bring one or more of the following:

- a weak and strong prompt pair
- an AI-generated summary with missing caveats
- a short example of an AI use note or disclosure paragraph

## Extension Design Principle

If time is limited, choose one richer case study with a strong debrief over several disconnected mini-tasks. In AI teaching, depth usually beats breadth.
